ucs.title: [LLM] MirrorFace (Earth Kasha) NOOPDOOR/LODEINFO DLL sideloading via legitimate signed Japanese-vendor binaries
ucs.description: Hunts for MirrorFace's signature execution chain where a legitimate, code-signed binary (JustSystems ATOK, K7Computing, Canon, Trend Micro, VMware) is dropped to a writable directory (Temp/ProgramData/AppData/Public) and side-loads a malicious DLL such as jsfc.dll, K7SysMn1.dll, or vftrace.dll that decrypts NOOPDOOR/LODEINFO/ANEL. This precise abuse of Japanese ISV binaries is the documented MirrorFace tradecraft used against Japanese MoFA, defense and academic targets (JPCERT/CC, Trend Micro, ESET reporting 2023-2025).
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as process_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("jsschhlp.exe","JSESPR.exe","K7SysMon.exe","K7TSMain.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","vftrace.exe","MsMpEng.exe","icsys.icn.exe","NDR.exe","vsls-agent.exe")) AND (Processes.process_path="*\\Users\\Public\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\AppData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\ProgramData\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\Windows\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process_path="*\\PerfLogs\\*") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | join type=inner dest process_id [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(Filesystem.file_path) as dropped_dll values(Filesystem.file_name) as dll_name from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_name IN ("jsfc.dll","K7SysMn1.dll","vftrace.dll","vmtools.dll","NDR.dll","shimgvw.dll","mpsvc.dll","libcurl.dll") by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Filesystem)`] | where firstTime > relative_time(now(),"-30d@d") | table firstTime dest user process_name process_path dll_name dropped_dll parent
ucs.kql:
let suspect_paths = dynamic([@"\users\public\", @"\appdata\", @"\programdata\", @"\windows\temp\", @"\perflogs\"]);
let mirrorface_loaders = dynamic(["jsschhlp.exe","jsesprjc.exe","k7sysmon.exe","k7tsmain.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","vftrace.exe","icsys.icn.exe","ndr.exe","vsls-agent.exe"]);
let mirrorface_dlls = dynamic(["jsfc.dll","k7sysmn1.dll","vftrace.dll","ndr.dll","shimgvw.dll","mpsvc.dll","vmtools.dll"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName has_any (mirrorface_loaders)
| where FolderPath has_any (suspect_paths)
| extend loader_pid = ProcessId
| project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, loader_pid
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName in~ (mirrorface_dlls) or (FolderPath has_any (suspect_paths) and FileName endswith ".dll")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, LoadedDll=FileName, LoadedDllPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessId
) on DeviceId, $left.loader_pid == $right.InitiatingProcessId
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "FileCreated"
    | where FileName endswith ".dll" or FileName endswith ".exe"
    | project DropTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DroppedFile=FileName, DroppedPath=FolderPath, InitiatingProcessFileName_drop=InitiatingProcessFileName
) on DeviceId
| where DroppedPath has_any (suspect_paths)
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, LoadedDll, LoadedDllPath, DroppedFile, DroppedPath, InitiatingProcessFileName_drop
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: The specific binary+DLL pairings (jsschhlp.exe + jsfc.dll, K7SysMon.exe + K7SysMn1.dll, vftrace) are publicly documented MirrorFace/Earth Kasha sideloading chains used to stage NOOPDOOR and LODEINFO. JustSystems ATOK is a Japanese-only IME — its presence in Public/Temp on a non-Japanese host, or a corporate host without ATOK installed, is virtually pathognomonic for this actor and not seen in comm

ucs.title: [LLM] MirrorFace abuse of Visual Studio Code Remote Tunnels (code.exe tunnel) for covert C2
ucs.description: Detects MirrorFace's 2024-2025 living-off-the-cloud tradecraft where the actor drops portable code.exe and registers a VS Code Remote Tunnel to *.devtunnels.ms / vscode.dev, giving operators interactive shell access through a Microsoft-signed channel that bypasses most C2 inspection. This was first attributed to MirrorFace by JPCERT/CC and Trend Micro targeting Japanese diplomatic and academic networks.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.process_path) as path values(Processes.user) as user from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name="code.exe" AND (Processes.process="*tunnel*" OR Processes.process="*--accept-server-license-terms*" OR Processes.process="*serve-web*") by Processes.dest Processes.process_id | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count values(All_Traffic.dest) as dest_ip values(Web.url) as url from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where (All_Traffic.dest="*.devtunnels.ms" OR All_Traffic.dest="*.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com" OR All_Traffic.dest="global.rel.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com") by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(All_Traffic)`] | where (path NOT LIKE "%Program Files%Microsoft VS Code%" AND path NOT LIKE "%AppData\\Local\\Programs\\Microsoft VS Code%") OR isnotnull(dest_ip) | eval anomaly=if(match(parent,"(?i)cmd\.exe|powershell\.exe|wmiprvse\.exe|services\.exe|rundll32\.exe|regsvr32\.exe"),"suspicious_parent","check") | table firstTime dest user path cmdline parent dest_ip anomaly
ucs.kql:
let tunnel_domains = dynamic(["devtunnels.ms","tunnels.api.visualstudio.com","global.rel.tunnels.api.visualstudio.com"]);
let legit_vscode_paths = dynamic([@"\program files\microsoft vs code\", @"\appdata\local\programs\microsoft vs code\"]);
// Process-side signal: code.exe spawning the tunnel command, or running from a non-standard path
let proc_signal = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName =~ "code.exe"
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any ("tunnel","serve-web","--accept-server-license-terms")
        or not(FolderPath has_any (legit_vscode_paths))
    | extend suspicious_parent = iff(InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wmiprvse.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","services.exe","mshta.exe"), true, false)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, suspicious_parent;
// Network-side signal: outbound to devtunnels infrastructure
let net_signal = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (tunnel_domains) or RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (tunnel_domains)
    | project NetTime=Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessAccountName;
proc_signal
| join kind=leftouter (net_signal) on DeviceId
| where suspicious_parent == true or isnotempty(RemoteUrl) or not(FolderPath has_any (legit_vscode_paths))
| extend Severity = case(suspicious_parent==true and isnotempty(RemoteUrl), "High", isnotempty(RemoteUrl), "Medium", "Low")
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FolderPath, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, Severity
| sort by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: VS Code Remote Tunnel abuse for C2 was first publicly attributed to MirrorFace/Earth Kasha by JPCERT/CC (Jan 2025) and Trend Micro. Legitimate developers run code.exe from %ProgramFiles% or %LocalAppData%\Programs\Microsoft VS Code; MirrorFace drops a portable build under ProgramData/Public and invokes `code.exe tunnel`, then operates over devtunnels.ms. Filtering by parent process (cmd/powershell
